1.

-----+------------+------------+-------------+
| **AF | FAP | 1 | 46,5 | 1,25 | 47,5 |
| 488** | | | | | |
+-------+------+------------+------------+------------+-------------+

1. Use the fast temperature (FT) function to cool down the centrifuge
 > (found in Vähärautio lab).

2. Keep samples on ice and shielded from light as much as possible.

3. Keep buffers on ice or in +4°C lab fridge until use.

4. Depending on the final cell yield, aliquot up to 1.5 x E6 cells per
 > Eppendorf tube per condition. Usually, 1x multicolor for each
 > dissociation condition, 1x unstained from one dissociation
 > condition, and then FMOs depending on the need and cell
 > availability. At least, DCM-FMO is recommended.

5. Wash cells once by adding 900 µL FACS buffer (2 % FBS, 2 mM EDTA in
 > PBS, stored in the wet lab fridge), centrifuge 6 min 400 g at
 > +4°C, and discard the supernatant.

 a. You may increase to 450 g if a pellet is not forming.

6. Prepare antibody mixes while centrifuging the samples: Surface
 > antibody staining

 a. For multicolor samples and a-SMA and PDGFRb FMO samples:

 i. Calculate the required amounts of antibodies (see table
 > above).

 ii. Prepare an antibody mix by diluting them in Brilliant Stain
 > Buffer (#563794, BD Biosciences, stored in the wet lab
 > fridge).

 iii. NOTE! Add the DCM last, just before pipetting into the
 > samples.

by diluting them in Brilliant Stain
 > Buffer (#563794, BD Biosciences, stored in the wet lab
 > fridge).

 iii. NOTE! Add the DCM last, just before pipetting into the
 > samples. DCM is not an antibody and may damage other
 > antibodies if stored in the mix for too long.

 iv. Flow antibodies found in "Antibodies for FACS box 2" in +4°C
 > wet lab fridge. Return the box immediately after use,
 > keeping the original antibody order. DCM can be found in
 > -20°C wet lab freezer.

 v. Add 50 µL of the antibody mix to each sample.

 b. For FMO samples:

 i. Add the required amount of Brilliant Stain Buffer to each
 > sample (see table above).

 ii. Add each antibody individually, one at a time, excluding the
 > antibody corresponding to the fluorophore being tested in
 > the FMO sample.

 c. Mix the antibodies and cells well with a pipette before starting
 > the incubation.

 d. Incubate for 30 min at RT shielded from light.

7. After incubation, wash by adding 900 µL FACS buffer, centrifuge 6
 > min 400 g at +4°C, and discard supernatant.

8. Fix cells by adding 200 µL IC Fixation buffer (#00-8222, Invitrogen,
 > stored in the wet lab fridge) and incubate for 40 min at +4°C
 > shielded from light.

9. After incubation, add 1 mL of 1x Permeabilization buffer
 > (#00-8333-56, Invitrogen, diluted in MQ water, stored in the wet
 > lab fridge) and incubate for 5 min at +4°C.

10.

9. After incubation, add 1 mL of 1x Permeabilization buffer
 > (#00-8333-56, Invitrogen, diluted in MQ water, stored in the wet
 > lab fridge) and incubate for 5 min at +4°C.

10. Centrifuge 6 min 400 g at +4°C, and discard supernatant.

11. Wash the pellet by adding 1 mL 1x Permeabilization buffer,
 > centrifuge 6 min 400 g at +4°C, discard supernatant, and resuspend
 > in 900 µL FACS buffer for overnight storage.

12. Next day, start by centrifuging 6 min 400 g at +4°C and discard
 > supernatant.

13. Prepare antibody mixes while centrifuging the samples: Intracellular
 > antibody staining

 a. For multicolor samples and FMOs samples excluding a-SMA and
 > PDGFRb:

 i. Calculate the required amounts of antibodies (see table
 > above).

 ii. Prepare an antibody mix by diluting them in 1x
 > Permeabilization buffer.

 iii. Add 50 µL of the antibody mix to each sample.

 b. For a-SMA and PDGFRb FMO samples:

 i. Add the required amount of 1x Permeabilization buffer to
 > each sample (see table above).

 ii. Add the remaining antibody to the cells: add a-SMA antibody
 > to the PDGFRb FMO sample and PDGFRb antibody to the a-SMA
 > FMO sample.

 c. Mix the antibodies and cells well with a pipette before starting
 > the incubation.

 d. Incubate for 30 min at RT shielded from light.

14. After incubation, wash by adding 1 mL 1x Permeabilization buffer,

s well with a pipette before starting
 > the incubation.

 d. Incubate for 30 min at RT shielded from light.

14. After incubation, wash by adding 1 mL 1x Permeabilization buffer,
 > centrifuge 6 min 400 g at +4°C, and discard supernatant.

15. Resuspend the pellets in 400 µL FACS buffer.

16. Pipetted the cell suspension through a 5 mL Falcon polystyrene tube
 > with a cell-strainer cap (blue) (#352235 Corning). Avoid bubbles.

17. Keep the samples on ice and protected from light until running on
 > Fortessa.

18. After running the samples, export the data as FCS files to a hard
 > drive.

Prepare compensation controls

1. Use Falcon 5 mL polystyrene round-bottom tubes (white cap) (#352052
 > Corning).

2. Prepare one unstained and 8 stained ones, one for each fluorophore.

3. Use BD CompBeads Plus (7.5 µm) compensation beads (#560497, BD
 > Biosciences, stored in the wet lab fridge).

4. Vortex the bead vials and add one drop of both positive (BD
 > CompBeads AntiMouse IgG, #51-9006274) and negative (CompBeads
 > Negative Control #51-9006227) compensation beads to each tube.

 a. Note! Ensure that the drops have reached the bottom of the tube.

5. For stained compensation controls, add 2 µL of each antibody.

 a. Except for a-SMA, for which 2.5 µL should be added.

 b. DCM cannot be used for compensation controls, so it should be

ained compensation controls, add 2 µL of each antibody.

 a. Except for a-SMA, for which 2.5 µL should be added.

 b. DCM cannot be used for compensation controls, so it should be
 > replaced with an antibody labeled with the same fluorophore
 > (BV510). For example, BV510 CD16 (#563829, BD Biosciences).

6. Incubate for at least 30 min at +4°C shielded from light.

7. Resuspend the beads in 350 µL FACS buffer.

8. Keep the compensations on ice and protected from light until running
 > on Fortessa.

9. Before running the sample, these compensations should be used to
 > determine the PMT voltages for each fluorochrome.
